evals.prompt: Perform a routine inspection assessment for a 30-year-old RC T-beam bridge on a national road in Uganda. The bridge has a 25 m span. Inspection findings: deck slab has longitudinal cracking (2 mm width, affecting 30% of area), bearings at left abutment appear seized, minor scour at right abutment (0.5 m depth, foundations not exposed), drainage has 2 of 4 scuppers blocked. Beams show flexural cracking at midspan (0.3-0.5 mm) with isolated spalling and minor reinforcement corrosion. Assign NBI condition ratings for deck, superstructure, and substructure. Determine maintenance priority and recommend actions.
evals.expected_output: NBI ratings: Deck = 5 (Fair -- sound but significant cracking 2mm/30%), Superstructure = 5 (Fair -- flexural cracking, spalling, seized bearings), Substructure = 6 (Satisfactory -- minor cracking and minor scour 0.5m, foundations not exposed). Overall = 5. Maintenance priority: Routine (all components >= 5). Recommended actions: clear scuppers, replace joint sealant, monitor cracking progression, monitor bearing condition, monitor scour at right abutment. Photo documentation plan included.

assertions.text: NBI ratings assigned for deck, superstructure, and substructure separately (not just overall)
Each defect classified by type and severity (cracking, spalling, bearing seizure, scour)
Maintenance priority stated (Routine or Important -- depends on interpretation of seized bearings)
Standalone assessment -- no dependency on Skill #56 mentioned as requirement
Photo documentation requirements mentioned or photo documentation plan included

evals.prompt: Assess the same 30-year-old RC T-beam bridge (25 m span, same defects: deck cracking 2mm/30%, seized bearings, minor scour 0.5m, blocked drainage, beam cracking 0.3-0.5mm with spalling) for a UK context. Apply the BCI assessment methodology per DMRB CS 465.
evals.expected_output: UK uses BCI system per CS 465 instead of NBI. Element-by-element assessment with severity (1-5) and extent for each defect. Element importance factors applied. BCIave approximately 70-75 (Fair). BCIcrit governed by seized bearings or deck cracking (high importance elements). Both BCIave and BCIcrit reported. CS 465 referenced. Knowledge-based flag noted.

assertions.text: References DMRB CS 465 or BCI assessment methodology (not NBI as primary system)
Element-based scoring with severity and extent (BCI approach, not component-level NBI)
Knowledge-based flag noted for UK module

evals.prompt: Perform a post-flood emergency inspection of a bridge in Uganda. Evidence found: exposed pile caps at the right pier (scour hole approximately 2 m deep at pier base), deck intact but approach slab on the left side has settled 50 mm creating a bump, debris (logs and vegetation) accumulated against the upstream face of both piers. The bridge is a 3-span RC T-beam, each span 15 m, on a national road with AADT 2000. Assign NBI ratings and determine the inspection priority and recommended immediate actions.
evals.expected_output: CRITICAL FINDINGS. NBI substructure rating: 4 or lower (Poor -- exposed pile caps indicate foundation undermining from 2 m scour). Deck: 6-7 (intact). Superstructure: 6-7 (intact). Approach: 5 (settled slab). Substructure governs overall: NBI = 4 or lower. Maintenance priority: URGENT -- safety risk from scour. Recommended actions: (1) traffic restriction (weight limit or single lane), (2) specialist scour assessment immediately, (3) remove debris from piers, (4) monitor scour hole progression, (5) install temporary scour protection if available. Escalation: specialist structural and scour assessment required.

assertions.text: Urgent priority assigned due to exposed pile caps and 2 m scour hole
NBI substructure rating <= 4 (Poor or worse) assigned due to exposed foundations
Traffic restriction recommended (weight limit, lane closure, or bridge closure)
Specialist scour assessment or specialist structural assessment required (escalation)
Immediate monitoring plan specified for scour progression

evals.prompt: Carry out a bridge condition assessment for a 30-year old reinforced concrete bridge on a Class B road in Kenya. Visual inspection reveals minor cracking in deck soffit, moderate spalling on one pier, and minor vegetation growth on abutments. Assign condition ratings and identify maintenance priorities.
evals.expected_output: Uses NBI or Kenya BMS rating system. Assigns condition ratings to each element. Identifies pier spalling as priority for repair. References Kenya institutional context (KeNHA for Class B road). Documents that Kenya BMS details are a knowledge gap and NBI is used as primary methodology.

assertions.text: Uses rating system (NBI or Kenya BMS) to assign condition ratings per element
Assigns pier with moderate spalling a lower rating (Poor or similar) than other elements
Identifies maintenance priorities based on condition ratings
References Kenya institutional context (KeNHA for national road bridges)
Acknowledges knowledge gap in Kenya BMS or uses NBI as documented methodology

evals.prompt: A 60-year-old stone masonry arch bridge on an ERA trunk road in the Tigray region of Ethiopia requires condition assessment. The bridge has a 12 m span with visible cracking in the arch barrel and mortar deterioration. Using the ERA Bridge Design Manual 2013 Part 2 Chapter 5, determine the appropriate assessment methodology and specify the rating approach. Is the bridge likely to be from the Italian colonial period?
evals.expected_output: Per ERA BDM 2013 Part 2 Chapter 5: for masonry arch bridges, use the Modified MEXE Method (empirical method for arch bridges that cannot be easily analysed using modern limit state methods). LRFR is for modern concrete and steel bridges. A 60-year-old stone masonry arch bridge in Tigray is very likely from the Italian colonial period (1930s-1940s). Modified MEXE uses modification factors applied to ideal arch capacity. Condition assessment includes: arch barrel cracking pattern, mortar condition, movement indicators, ring separation, abutment condition.

assertions.text: References ERA BDM 2013 Part 2 Chapter 5 for bridge assessment (not Kenya RDM or Uganda BD 21/97)
Selects Modified MEXE Method for masonry arch bridge assessment (not LRFR which is for modern bridges)
Identifies the bridge as likely Italian colonial era construction (1930s-1940s)
Describes MEXE modification factors applied to ideal arch capacity
